SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.fire

Class PfpDemandCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.fire.PfpDemandCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class PfpDemandCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Passive fire protection (PFP) demand calculator per API 521 / NORSOK S-001.

Screens whether an exposed steel wall reaches its critical (allowable) temperature before the required survival time under fire exposure, and if not, estimates the conduction-limited PFP (e.g. intumescent epoxy) thickness required to meet the survival time.

Method (lumped steel wall):

  • Bare-steel time to critical temperature: t_bare = m″·Cp·ΔT / q_abs, with steel mass per area m″ = ρ_steel·thickness and absorbed flux q_abs = α·q_inc
  • If t_bare ≥ t_req, no PFP is required
  • Otherwise, the allowable steady heat input to still reach ΔT only at t_req is q_allow = m″·Cp·ΔT / t_req, and the conduction-limited PFP thickness is L = k_pfp·(T_fire − T_steel,avg) / q_allow
The required rating is mapped from the fire type and survival time (jet → "J", pool/hydrocarbon → "H"; duration 60 or 120 min → H60/H120/J60).

References: API STD 521 §4.5 (fire heat input) and §5.20 (depressuring), NORSOK S-001 (Technical safety), ISO 22899-1 (jet-fire resistance test), UL 1709 / H-class hydrocarbon fire curve.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

    PfpDemandCalculator
    setAbsorptivity(double alpha)
    Set the steel surface absorptivity (default 0.9).
    PfpDemandCalculator
    setCriticalTemperatureK(double tK)
    Set the critical (allowable) steel temperature (default 673.15 K ≈ 400 °C, common UTS-degradation limit).
    PfpDemandCalculator
    setFireTemperatureK(double tK)
    Set the effective fire (flame) temperature used for the PFP conduction calc (default 1173.15 K ≈ 900 °C).
    PfpDemandCalculator
    setFireType(PfpDemandCalculator.FireType fireType)
    Set the fire type (default POOL).

    Set the initial wall temperature (default 288.15 K).
    PfpDemandCalculator
    setPfpConductivityWPerMK(double kWPerMK)
    Set the PFP material thermal conductivity (default 0.2 W/(m·K), representative intumescent epoxy).

  • Constructor Details

    • PfpDemandCalculator

      public PfpDemandCalculator(double incidentHeatFluxWPerM2, double steelThicknessM)
      Construct a PFP demand calculator.
      Parameters:
      incidentHeatFluxWPerM2 - incident fire heat flux on the wall in W/m² (API 521: ~100 kW/m² pool, up to ~350 kW/m² jet)
      steelThicknessM - exposed steel wall thickness in m

  • Method Details

    • setAbsorptivity

      public PfpDemandCalculator setAbsorptivity(double alpha)
      Set the steel surface absorptivity (default 0.9).
      Parameters:
      alpha - absorptivity in (0,1]
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining

    • setInitialTemperatureK

      public PfpDemandCalculator setInitialTemperatureK(double tK)
      Set the initial wall temperature (default 288.15 K).
      Parameters:
      tK - initial temperature in K
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining

    • setCriticalTemperatureK

      public PfpDemandCalculator setCriticalTemperatureK(double tK)
      Set the critical (allowable) steel temperature (default 673.15 K ≈ 400 °C, common UTS-degradation limit).
      Parameters:
      tK - critical temperature in K
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining

    • setFireTemperatureK

      public PfpDemandCalculator setFireTemperatureK(double tK)
      Set the effective fire (flame) temperature used for the PFP conduction calc (default 1173.15 K ≈ 900 °C).
      Parameters:
      tK - fire temperature in K
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining

      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining

      public double bareSteelTimeToCriticalS()
      Bare-steel time to reach the critical temperature under the incident fire flux.

      time to critical temperature in s

    • evaluate

      public PfpDemandCalculator.PfpDemandResult evaluate(double requiredSurvivalTimeS)
      Evaluate the PFP demand for a required survival (fire endurance) time.
      Parameters:
      requiredSurvivalTimeS - the required survival time in s (e.g. 3600 for 60 min)
      Returns:
      the PFP demand result

    • selectRating

      private PfpDemandCalculator.PfpRating selectRating(boolean pfpRequired, double requiredSurvivalTimeS)
      Select the PFP rating from the fire type and required survival time.
      Parameters:
      pfpRequired - whether PFP is required
      requiredSurvivalTimeS - required survival time in s
      Returns:
      the PFP rating
